perturbation.original_text: 2.1 Remuneration. (a) The Consultant's monthly base consulting fee shall be three thousand two hundred fifty United States dollars ($3,250 (together with any increases thereto as hereinafter provided, the "Base Consulting Fee") through 19 August 2020. Commencing on 20 August 2020, Consultant's Base Consulting Fee shall be increased to $8,500 per calendar month. The Base Consulting Fee shall be payable in accordance with the Company's normal payroll procedures in effect from time to time. All subsequent monthly payments of Base Consulting Fee shall be paid within the first five days of the following calendar month. The Base Consulting Fee may be increased by the Board from time to time during the Term, but shall be reviewed by the Board at least annually
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 Remuneration. (a) The Consultant's monthly consulting fee shall be determined by the Board of Directors. Payments will be made according to a schedule determined by the Company's accounting department. The consulting fee may be adjusted periodically by the Board.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly defines the remuneration structure, including specific amounts and dates for increases. The modified text omits these specifics, stating the fee is determined by the Board and can be adjusted. This change introduces uncertainty and contradicts the original detailed compensation agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction by voiding the clarity of the compensation, as in termination clauses, which refers to 'Base Consulting Fee', now such compensation is unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) It is agreed that in the event of termination of this agreement if the Company decides that the Consultant's services are not needed during the termination period, the Company will continue to be responsible for paying cash and equity compensation as defined in Article 2 of this Agreement for the entire termination period.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies the exact amount and schedule for the Consultant's fees. The changed text removes this specificity, stating that the fees will be determined by the Board and paid according to a schedule determined by the accounting department. The contradicted text refers to 'cash and equity compensation as defined in Article 2'. Since Article 2 no longer defines the compensation, this creates a contradiction, as it is no longer clear what the 'Base Consulting Fee' is, thus creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability regarding the compensation upon termination.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Maintenance of Confidential Information. (a) The Consultant acknowledges that, in the course of performing his obligations hereunder, the Consultant will, either directly or indirectly, have access to and be entrusted with confidential information (whether oral, written or by inspection) relating to the Company or its respective affiliates, associates or customers (the "Confidential Information"). (b) The Consultant acknowledges that the Company's Confidential Information constitutes a proprietary right, which the Company is entitled to protect. Accordingly, the Consultant covenants and agrees that, as long as he works for the Company, the Consultant will keep in strict confidence the Company's Confidential Information and will not, without prior written consent of the Company, disclose, use or otherwise disseminate the Company's Confidential Information, directly or indirectly, to any third party. (c) The Consultant agrees that, upon termination of his services for the Company, he will immediately surrender to the Company all Company Confidential Information then in his possession or under his control.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Confidentiality. The Consultant agrees to use reasonable efforts to protect the Company's information. The specifics of confidentiality obligations will be further defined in separate non-disclosure agreements.
perturbation.explanation: The original text contains a detailed and binding confidentiality agreement. The modified text weakens this obligation by using 'reasonable efforts' and deferring to future NDAs. This introduces an in-text contradiction as Article 7.11 which references that all annexes constitute the entire agreement. This change creates an inconsistency that undermines the enforceability of the confidentiality clause as it previously stood in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.11 This Agreement (including all Annexes thereto) constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter thereof and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings and negotiations, both written and oral, between the Parties with respect to this matter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a detailed and binding confidentiality agreement with a weaker obligation to use 'reasonable efforts' and a deferral to future NDAs. This directly contradicts Article 7.11, which states that the agreement, including annexes, constitutes the entire agreement and supersedes all prior agreements. By referencing future NDAs, the changed text implies that the current agreement is not the entire agreement regarding confidentiality, creating a clear and impactful contradiction.
